item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Blaseball, is that you?

Not to put either one down, but as soon as I heard about Blaseball I thought about this SCP entry.

Differences include:
…
• On-field fights between opposing teams are common, expected, and considered a legitimate manner of contesting an umpire's call.
• Serious injuries and fatalities are significantly more common.
…
• Players are highly divergent from baseline humans. Genetically-modified and cybernetically-enhanced humans, sapient ectomorphs, androids, and other anomalous humanoids are present in some combination on all teams.
• Fan superstitions appear to be capable of actually affecting the outcome of a game.

If I was going to try to bring SCP-2206 to life, Blaseball is how'd I'd have done it.

• The creation of a fictional website for SCP-2206, describing it as a radio show parodying popular sports talk radio.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not my fault there are too many baseball teams.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite the fact that the table here definitely goes on for too long and would benefit from having three or four entries cut out, the entries are immensely entertaining and I can comfortably upvote in spite of that concern.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a Rockies fan, I loved the nod to their home field advantage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Relevant to this: noted sports publication The Onion has some rather controversial opinions regarding shapeshifting during Maximum League Baseball games. Personally, I think that it's horses#!t that the Cleveland Exiles can win half their games by changing into Peregrine Falcons every at-bat.

E: Also congrats on +300!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Alright! Thanks for your feedback. We'll move forward with the tale link only.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Gonna have to agree with this. While the crosslink to the tale works fine, the other crosslinks don't work as well as I thought they would when you first suggested them to me.

Given the nature of the skip, it would be very easy to shoehorn in a bunch of crosslinks to really any other skip that has ghosts, robots, drugs, etc…, but that wouldn't really be a good track to take. That said, if people want to propose other potential crosslinks, I can maybe try to work them in if they fit. There's definitely room for a crosslink to something about memetic engineering, and probably one about multiversal travel or transmissions.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
To be frank, the crosslinks aren't necessary and look somewhat shoehorned in. Including the tale displaying a sample of sports commentary is obviously fitting to the article and I'm totally in favor of that one, a link to an Anderson Robotics 'droid and an anomalous drug look like non sequiturs to me. There's no telling if the performance enhancing drugs in this universe are anomalous in nature, and linking an otherwise random article that happens to be an android (not to mention the somber tone of said article compared to this one) breaks the flow and otherwise kills the mood. Lastly, I'm not sure what an article about ghostly lightbulbs has to do with a team consisting of ghost athletes.

Anyway, that's my opinion on the matter. I feel they break up the tone more than they add to it.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below, per author stipulations. The author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. Maximum League Baseball. A true masterpiece of mixing weirdness and comedy. There are ample opportunities to in-line link through out this skip. The mention of most of the players being on performance enhancers can be linked to SCP-1853, which could easily be the means of achieving this end in the world of the MLB. Additionally, the mention of a team using androids and a team made up of Class A Ectomorphs can be linked to SCP-1360 and SCP-2176 respectfully, since the former is a prototypical android skip (plus AR in the MLB universe may very well supply said androids) and the later is where the term "class A ectomorph" makes its first appearance. Additionally, a link tto the tale Walk in the Park can be made since it is, in fact, a tale about SCP-2206. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

The use of steroids and other performance-enhancing drugs is mandatory.

To this:

The use of steroids and other performance-enhancing drugs is mandatory.

Changing this:

Players are highly divergent from baseline humans. Genetically-modified and cybernetically-enhanced humans, sapient ectomorphs, androids, and other anomalous humanoids are present in some combination on all teams.

To this:

Players are highly divergent from baseline humans. Genetically-modified and cybernetically-enhanced humans, sapient ectomorphs, androids, and other anomalous humanoids are present in some combination on all teams.

Changing this:

Believed to be Class-A Ectomorphs based on SCP-2206 commentary.

To this:

Believed to be Class-A Ectomorphs based on SCP-2206 commentary.

Changing this:

The content of SCP-2206 is a sports commentary and discussion show. While the focus of this show is a sport referred to as baseball, this sport is significantly different from any known version of baseball played on Earth.

To this:

The content of SCP-2206 is a sports commentary and discussion show. While the focus of this show is a sport referred to as baseball, this sport is significantly different from any known version of baseball played on Earth.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the real MLB was like this, I'd basically never stop watching.

item.title: Funny and well done!
item.content:encoded: Finally, a universe where baseball isn't boring! Well written and humorous. Got my upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As great as this is, my favorite part is probably MTF Lambda-30, who presumably joined the Foundation to fight monsters, and now basically host a podcast. Where is the shitposting MTF created to fight Gamers Against Weed??

It's pretty intriguing actually! Most Agents are probably recruited from the military, did the Foundation draft a bunch of improvisers for Lambda-30? A bunch of goofs think they're going into an SNL audition, and instead they get handed a big file about a crazy baseball show and told that the fate of the world could depend on them doing a good enough impression of the hosts.

Or maybe the MTF is made out of the researchers responsible for the SCP, seeing as they know it the best. Either way, really made me think about the side of the Foundation I hadn't considered before!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Grand slam.
I nearly got kicked out of the library for laughing at this so hard. I hope you're happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The rules actually require the pitch to be aimed at the batter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The rules still require the pitch to be in that general direction?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably immortal as in "unable to be killed."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did the Foundation even HAVE a baseball terminology primer until after coming across this thing?

item.title: Happy Birthday Baseball
item.content:encoded:
It's so weird to think that this article is a year old now.

Not as weird as the Cubs winning the World Series though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is possible that in SCP-2206's universe, this is the state reptile of Maryland.

Aaaaand upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't forget that the suggestion that started this was that the Cardinals' cardinal Cardinal Cardinal was a cardinal, which would still fall under the cardinal for Cardinals and therefore a cardinal Cardinal is cardinal.

Edit, also the color, so:
The cardinal for Cardinals reminds us that that the Cardinals' cardinal cardinal cardinal Cardinal Cardinal is cardinal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this particular Cardinals' Cardinal Cardinal is cardinal, it suggests that there is a cardinal for multiple cardinal Cardinals, likely all cardinal Cardinals under the Cardinals. The cardinal for the Cardinals' Cardinal Cardinals reminds us whether any particular cardinal Cardinal is cardinal or not cardinal for the Cardinals.

(That's brilliant, sirpudding.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Cardinals' cardinal Cardinal Cardinal?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Technically it would be capitalized even if it was an avian member of the clergy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: their mascot is a human. That's why "Cardinal" is capitalized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you say that the Cardinals' mascot is a cardinal, do you mean they have a human cardinal, or a bird that is a Catholic cardinal?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another one in the "I know nothing about baseball, but this is pretty great" camp. I like how it escalates "the baseball is notably different, for example, the fields are larger, the bats are aluminium, on-field fights are allowed [like in hockey so not weird], and the players murder each other".

Although they share a name with our universe's St. Louis Cardinals, the SCP-2206 Cardinals have an actual Catholic Cardinal as their mascot, who is an ordained bishop of the Roman Catholic Church and a member of the College of Cardinals. This is in contrast to our universe's St. Louis Cardinals, who do not.

This is actually what made me laugh out loud at 2 AM, because a) the implication that this apparently needs affirming and b) realization that it actually does.

As for whether it's a hoax, it works either way: either some reality bleeds into ours (and it's an odd combination of cartoon logic of players being ghosts cursed to haunt a field until they stop losing and the reality that the curse is unbreakable because they can't play away games), or some guy can just willy-nilly set up a radio signal with untraceable source, and the Foundation's idea to contain it is to start advertising it and selling merchandise. In which case, I wonder if he sues for royalties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: LOL, nice. It sounds exactly how I'd picture baseball in Night Vale or somewhere :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of that SCP that I wished were real. I can see myself being a huge fan of this broadcast.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know those old Disney cartoons where a bunch of Goofies (except none of them were actually Goofy or something) played a sport like hockey or American football or the like, usually resulting in a bunch of slapstick and stupidity? For some reason, I can't help but think of that when I read this.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. Just had another read re: San Diego Whales. The magic still works. Thanks, Retian! ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at "Boston Red Shirts". Throw in some transcripts from the broadcasts and it's perfect.

item.title: fantastic
item.content:encoded: I usually just upvote and leave, but this article compelled me to add a comment. I am impressed by the amount of effort you put into this. This is far from being a -J, I never felt that way about it for one moment. Side note: As someone from Kansas City, I like how you recognized the city is in two states (I was born in KCK). +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done, and upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My sentiment exactly!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting here that MrAnakinSpecter beat me to the punch and wrote a tale about this. (Link). It's great, it's wonderful, and you should all go read it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll sum up my feelings in a line by line.

+ Now, for my critique
- Wait I was meant to be critiquing? I was too busy laughing my ass off

Currently holds the record for most fatalities sustained in a single game, set during a 1903 game against the New York Highlanders. After losing their entire roster, the Red Shirts began drafting spectators onto the team. They eventually won 1-0.

Laughs ass off

Detroit Wolverines

Members of the team appear to be immortal.

Dies of laughter

Cincinnati Communists

Between 1952 and 1993, the entire team was illegal under US law.

Is very dead

GreenWolf I fucking love you XD

item.title: Re: @ What Eta said.
item.content:encoded: Thanks to both of you for the compliments, and I'm glad you liked the skip. Personally, I'm just happy that I wrote something that people seem to enjoy.

item.title: Re: @ What Eta said.
item.content:encoded: I laugh so hard every time I read this whale thing that i'm starting to fear for my life…!

item.title: @ What Eta said.
item.content:encoded:
Yup, I had the exact same reaction when first reading this. Luckily I started reading it 'cause I was curious about it. After reading the article I was laughing out loud in front of the computer, so much that my friends came to see if every thing was alright.

It really is an amazing SCiP. Thank you so much GreenWolf for your work on this one ;-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<laughing so much I ache all over>
Okay. I remember seeing this when you first posted it. Read the first lines and went 'Oops. Baseball. Won't even try.' (I don't understand anything about baseball, and wouldn't if my life depended on it).
Now, after reading the very funny (and highly intriguing) secret santa gift you received about this skip, I decided to try anyway.

This has just jumped aaaaaaaaaaaall the way into the top of my top ten favourite skips.

Let's see… I laughed the most at this, I think:

Players have repeatedly been the targets of Japanese whaling ships, despite not actually being whales.

But this is close second:

The team's roster includes two self-driving cars, four unmanned aerial vehicles, and three autonomous vacuum cleaners. It is unknown how they are able to play baseball.

Oh, wait, this too… Oh, never mind. Everything about this is excellent!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From their inception, the entire team has been cursed to haunt their stadium as "ghosts"4 until they can go an entire season without losing a game. Their ability to complete this goal is hindered by their inability to play away games.

This is one of the few times I've laughed out loud while reading a scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this SCP so much. I just had to log in so I can upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"In lieu of an opening pitch, the team starts their home games by ritually sacrificing a B-list celebrity."

+1.

If I were a cleverer man, I would have had a gif of some sort prepared to post here. Perhaps a collection of screenshots of comments saying "I don't like baseball, but this is great".

Alas, I am not that clever.

So instead, you get this post.

Thanks for all the upvotes folks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's quite a lot of pop culture references there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go Terrapins!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although they are named after the Maryland state reptile, their mascot is apparently a three-ton, acid-spitting lizard capable of launching poisonous spines from its back. It is possible that in SCP-2206's reality, this is the state reptile of Maryland.

I think I might start a petition to make this the new Maryland state reptile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was gonna be a Brockian Ultra-Cricket League at first. And though it wasn't (to my disappointment), you really captured the Douglas Adam's vibe here. Good stuff.

Though if there aren't any balls/walks, what's the incentive for the pitcher to throw remotely near the batter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell for all the Foundation knows, the metrics are completely different in that universe. Maybe an alt!foot is only 3 inches here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always love me some extradimensional weirdness. Enthusiastic +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of, congratulations on being the first person to realize the fridge horror here. I was wondering how long that would take.

As for imperial units, the dimensions for baseball fields are traditionally given in feet. I figured that since all the info the Foundation has on this is coming from the commentary provided by 2206, it would be acceptable to quote the dimensions in imperial units, and give an estimate in metric.

Anyways, glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uhm, it appears I'm not the only one who thinks goofyness is great in a skip. And looking at this through fridge logic, I believe silly articles can be more horrifying than the articles who actually try to be horrifying, because how fucked up is this reality if this exists within? And in this skip's case, is the baseline universe that different from 2206's? At the end, nobody is safe. You could be spacing the hell out at work, and BAM! You're a sapient banana. Why? No reason at all. It's just how the universe works, and everyone has to deal with it.

Overall, the skip is pretty well done. Some of the teams are rather lackluster, but it's ok. Also, imperial units are bugging me. If you're describing the stadium's size, just go full on metrics man.

Anyways, you made me feel excitement towards baseball, and I laughed pretty hard, so I can overlook at that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know at this point any articles that applied this idea to a different sport would just come off as derivative, but I'd like to see them anyways if they were written this well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A decent amount of the inspiration for this actually came from wacky sports video games like the ones you mentioned, particularly Blood Bowl. Other sources of inspiration were the Cox 'n' Crendor podcast, Welcome to Nightvale (especially when coming up with potential containment procedures), and the XFL.

If anyone wants to take a stab at trying to make a video game based on this, go nuts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love everything about this. Reminds me a lot of various wacky sports video games, like Base Wars for NES, Mutant League Hockey for Genesis, and Sega Soccer Slam for PS2/GC/XB, only I want a video game based on this baseball league more than any of those.

My favorite excerpt was for the Chicago Orphans, but by default I would probably have to root for the Seattle Autopilots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The team's roster includes two self-driving cars, four unmanned aerial vehicles, and three autonomous vacuum cleaners. It is unknown how they are able to play baseball.

This one was my favorite, if just for the mental image of a homicidal roomba stealing third base.

Enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Humorous, but not so dry till it's a -J. Everything is well described, and there's a consistent image of how the games work and usually go. Even though I know nothing about baseball.

So a +1, just because of the sheer detail and lack of confusion I usually get from 'Alternate Universe' skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have plans for a tale/supplement broadcast transcript. So, expect that sometime between now and the heat death of the universe.
